summary: High‑energy 1977 Winterland show captured on a surprisingly clear audience tape, highlighted by scorching The Other One, Sugaree and Brokedown Palace performances.
review: Fans rave about the vibrant second set – especially the blistering The Other One, one of the best Sugaree renditions ever heard, and an emotional Brokedown Palace encore.  The audience (AUD) recording from ’77 is praised as “fantastic for an AUD,” with limited crowd bleed and enough low‑end to feel the Winterland space, making it a very listenable document despite not being a soundboard source.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive star ratings (average ≈ 4.7) and consistent praise for both the musical performance and the surprisingly good audience recording give this release a top‑tier rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear enough for an AUD from 1977; low‑end present, crowd noise appears only when needed, occasional unwanted noise but overall crisp and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A long, high‑energy second set that keeps the excitement rolling; fans point to a “hidden gem” quality with seamless transitions and an emotional encore.

band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's Moog work on Row Jimmy is singled out as “dope,” and overall keyboard texture receives praise.
